channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Scientists Identify a New Genus of Gliding Possum in Papua
item.description: Scientists have formally described a new genus of mammal, a gliding possum named Tous, from the forests of Papua, Indonesia. Previously believed to have gone extinct around 6,000 years ago, this speci...

item.title: Public Submits Humorous and Serious Names for UK Storms
item.description: The Met Office has received a wide variety of storm name suggestions from the public, including playful entries such as Elon Gust and Dame Judi Drench. This tradition of inviting public contributions ...

item.title: Challenges in implementing the US America First Global Health Strategy in Africa
item.description: The United States America First Global Health Strategy wey President Trump start for September 2025 dey face wahala as e no too dey work well for Africa. The plan na to provide direct health support t...

item.title: Rivers Residents Seek Government Action on Motorcycle-Related Violence
item.description: One group wey dey call themselves Concerned Citizens for Peace, Safety and Lawful Coexistence for Rivers State don carry matter go DSS and Nigeria Police Force, dey beg them make dem intervene as some...

item.title: Philippines Leads Effort to Establish ASEAN Regional Disaster Response Plan
item.description: The Philippines, as chair of ASEAN in 2026, is spearheading discussions for a regional disaster response framework called the Manila-ASEAN Strategic Protocol for Emergency and Comprehensive Transforma...

item.title: Legal Proceedings Continue in Frank Stronach Sexual Assault Case
item.description: In Toronto, legal proceedings are ongoing in the sexual assault trial of billionaire businessman Frank Stronach, founder of Magna International. On Friday afternoon, lawyers are scheduled to make lega...

item.title: Court allows South African gold mine to build solar plant despite Eskom opposition
item.description: In South Africa, Eskom, the state-owned electricity utility, has long dominated power supply, with coal-fired stations generating about 74% of the country’s electricity. However, the nation’s energy p...

item.title: How unequal public toilet design disadvantages women and reveals gender biases in urban planning
item.description: Public toilets in buildings such as theatres, airports, shopping centres, and festivals often see long queues for women's facilities, while men experience minimal waits. This disparity arises because ...

item.title: Selena Gomez playfully kisses Benny Blanco’s foot during podcast appearance
item.description: During a recent episode of Benny Blanco’s podcast 'Friends Keep Secrets,' Selena Gomez surprised listeners with a playful gesture by kissing her husband’s foot in the middle of a conversation. The 33-...

item.title: Rastriya Swatantra Party Takes Early Lead in Nepal General Election, Balendra Shah Surpasses Former PM Oli
item.description: In Nepal’s recent general elections, the newly-formed Rastriya Swatantra Party has emerged as a significant frontrunner, leading in 109 seats out of 275, while the Nepali Congress is ahead in 12 seats...

item.title: South African Creative Workers Push for Legal Recognition and Labour Rights
item.description: On 23 January 2026, Minister of Employment and Labour Nomakhosazana Meth announced plans to elevate the legal status of cultural and creative workers in South Africa, a move aimed at granting them ben...

item.title: Netflix series ‘Hello Bachhon’ showcases the journey of Physics Wallah founder Alakh Pandey
item.description: Netflix has released 'Hello Bachhon', a five-episode Hindi series portraying the story of Physics Wallah founder Alakh Pandey, who began with a humble YouTube channel and built it into a successful ed...

item.title: EAC finalizes preparations for Heads of State Summit amid economic challenges
item.description: The East African Community (EAC) held its 59th Extraordinary Meeting of the Council of Ministers in Arusha, Tanzania, as part of final preparations for the 25th Heads of State Summit scheduled for Mar...

item.title: Indonesia Implements Age Restrictions on Social Media for Users Under 16
item.description: Indonesia has announced new measures to limit social media access for children below 16 years old, as revealed by Communications and Digital Minister Meutya Hafid on March 6, 2026. Through a freshly i...

item.title: Apple Music Introduces AI Transparency Tags for Songs and Videos
item.description: Apple Music don announce say all record labels and music distributors must now show if dem use AI for their work. This new system wey dem call 'Transparency Tags' go cover four things: album artwork, ...

item.title: PM directs provinces to prevent petroleum hoarding and ensure supply
item.description: On Friday, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif chaired a high-level meeting regarding petroleum products and instructed provincial governments to take strict legal action against any individuals or petrol p...

item.title: Somali parliament approves constitutional amendments amid potential election delay
item.description: Somalia’s parliament has approved constitutional amendments that could potentially extend President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud’s term in office by a year, causing planned elections to be postponed. The vot...

item.title: Kerrie Stratford Wins 22nd Apple Peeling Title at Stanthorpe Festival
item.description: At the Stanthorpe Apple and Grape Harvest Festival in Queensland, Kerrie Stratford, 65, defended her title as the champion apple peeler, securing her 22nd win. Despite living with chronic pain from De...

item.title: Western Cape High Court Rules Only Parliament Can Change VAT Rate
item.description: The Western Cape High Court has declared a section of the Value Added Tax Act unconstitutional, ruling that only Parliament has the authority to introduce, increase, or reduce national taxes. Judge Ma...

item.title: Seven Nigeria Police AIGs Clear Promotion Screening, To Be Elevated To DIGs
item.description: Seven Assistant Inspectors-General of Police (AIGs) for Nigeria don pass their promotion screening to become Deputy Inspectors-General of Police (DIGs). Disu, the Inspector-General of Police, go perso...
